action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py with per-target custom camera rays using rel_az works on val (completed all objs, correct RGBA saves), but crashes during eval on test set after processing a few objects (around uid starting with 02877... at view_10). The printed cameras show some extreme elevations close to +/-90 and various az. LPIPS not updated yet (still 0.6121 from old orbit render). Analysis: The root cause is likely numerical instability in get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth for certain az/elev combinations (cross product =0 when lookat // up vector, leading to nan after normalize despite eps, then renderer fails with nan input or exception). The parallel case happens e.g. when lookat aligns with z-axis (elev=0, az=0 or 180). The test val didn't hit those exact params but test does. The custom pose matching is correct approach and should give significantly better LPIPS once stable. Decision: Update the get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth with robust up-vector selection (switch to different axis if dot product too high indicating parallel), improve tensor handling, add device to all, add empty_cache and gc after each object, make render output to RGBA more robust with multiple possible output key names and fallback to model.render() on error. Test on full val set first (to confirm no crash on all variety), then submit_eval. This will get the improved score and time.
